summary: All 5 QA-designed scenarios pass on an independent scratch repo. The rewritten algorithm correctly keeps the NEWEST RETENTION commits (not the oldest), preserves tip tree SHA, correctly chains via commit-tree, is idempotent, handles extreme retention (=1), and survives git fsck. Anti-regression checks confirm L1-L5, O1, O2, O4, R1 still intact.

test_results.name: original failure scenario (206 commits, RETENTION=200)
test_results.script_output: refs/checkpoints/master: 206 -> 200 commits (rewrote chain, new tip b35c6283192a). pruned=1 skipped=0 failed=0

measured.git_fsck_output: dangling commit 51e659eedf... (expected: the original tip is now unreachable after chain rewrite; 'dangling' is informational, not an error)

test_results.notes: new_tip_sha differs from original_tip_sha by design — the algorithm rebuilds the chain via git commit-tree, so every kept commit gets a new SHA. What matters for correctness is that the tip's TREE SHA is identical (verified), guaranteeing the ref exposes the same snapshot content. The rebuilt tip's message + author + committer metadata are preserved from the original tip (verified separately).

test_results.name: boundary (3 commits, RETENTION=5) — expect no-op
test_results.script_output: refs/checkpoints/master: 3 commits (<= 5) — no pruning needed. pruned=0 skipped=1 failed=0. no pruning performed — skipping gc
test_results.notes: Ref SHA is bit-identical before and after; confirms the no-op path is a true no-op (no ref mutation, no gc invocation).

test_results.name: idempotency (re-run prune on already-pruned 200-commit ref, RETENTION=200)
test_results.script_output: refs/checkpoints/master: 200 commits (<= 200) — no pruning needed. pruned=0 skipped=1 failed=0
test_results.notes: Second run is a clean no-op: ref untouched, count stays at RETENTION, gc skipped.

test_results.name: extreme retention (10 commits, RETENTION=1)
test_results.script_output: refs/checkpoints/master: 10 -> 1 commits (rewrote chain, new tip f188c432c791). pruned=1 skipped=0 failed=0
test_results.deviation_from_prompt: The prompt said 'SHA == original tip'. Strictly speaking the NEW tip SHA is different because the algorithm recreates the commit via commit-tree (new parent set = empty instead of original's parent). However the TREE SHA, MESSAGE, AUTHOR, and COMMITTER metadata are all preserved from the original tip. A SHA-identical result is impossible for any algorithm that orphans an existing commit (removing a commit's parent necessarily changes its SHA). The semantic intent — 'keep only the newest commit with its content' — is satisfied.
test_results.notes: Single-commit chain is correctly parentless (orphan root). Tree SHA identical to original tip's tree. Message 'ck 9' (the newest) preserved.

test_results.name: tree content preservation across rewrite
test_results.method: git cat-file -p refs/checkpoints/master:file.txt on the post-Test-1 repo (200 commits, ck 6..ck 205 kept)
test_results.notes: The tip exposes the ORIGINAL newest snapshot ('content 205'), proving the keeping direction is correct. Each intermediate commit retains its own original tree (e.g. the oldest-kept commit's tree shows 'content 6'), so chain reachability walks through meaningful distinct snapshots, not a flattened chain.

metadata_preservation_verification.evidence: Inspected oldest-kept ('ck 6') and newest ('ck 205') commits in the post-Test-1 repo. Both show author 'QA Tester <qa@test.local>' with their original ISO-8601 author dates. The script uses `git log -1 --format='%aI'` and `%cI` to extract dates then re-injects via GIT_AUTHOR_DATE/GIT_COMMITTER_DATE. Message uses `git log -1 --format='%B' >msgfile` + `git commit-tree -F msgfile` to preserve multi-line bodies safely.

regression_checks_performed.check: L1 hooks/ensure-git-repo.sh still a 18-line exit-0 stub, zero 'git commit' matches
L2 hooks/session-git-init.sh HEAD guard still at line 85 before git commit at line 91
L3 commands/clean.md still delegates to checkpoint.sh, zero 'git commit/add' matches
L4 commands/test.md still delegates to checkpoint.sh, zero 'git commit/add' matches
L5 hooks/git-fswatch.sh safe_add still removed (only comment lines 263-264 remain)
O1 /etc/logrotate.d/claude-checkpoint exists and logrotate -d parses cleanly (exit 0)

O2 hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh still contains CHECKPOINT_FAIL_COUNT_FILE (line 70), CHECKPOINT_FAIL_ALERT_THRESHOLD (line 71), _checkpoint_record_failure (line 96), _checkpoint_record_success (line 112), and CHECKPOINT ALERT string (line 107)
R1 git fsck on production /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude returns exit 0 with zero error lines

script_quality.concurrency_safety: CAS update-ref with original tip as expected old value — aborts cleanly if a concurrent writer has advanced the ref between snapshot and update
script_quality.sanity_check: Rebuilt tip's tree SHA must equal original tip's tree SHA before update-ref is called; mismatch aborts the rebuild and leaves the ref untouched
script_quality.portability: Uses awk instead of GNU-only tac; mktemp for message file; standard POSIX features in bash under 'set -euo pipefail'
script_quality.idempotency: Verified by Test 3 — rerun on already-pruned ref is a bit-identical no-op

app_understanding_flow.skip_reason: Bash/CLI infrastructure change — no browser-accessible application surface. All verification performed via direct script execution on scratch repos.

hardcode_scan_results.notes: RETENTION, REFLOG_EXPIRE, GC_PRUNE all take env overrides with documented defaults (200, 30.days, 30.days.ago). No absolute paths. No URLs. No credentials. Script uses $PWD and $REPO_ROOT from git-discovered values.

band_aid_detection.notes: Fix is a full algorithm replacement, not a threshold weakening or error-swallow. The prior safety check (merge-base --is-ancestor, which masked the semantic inversion) has been replaced with a correctness-focused check (tree-SHA equivalence at tip). No exceptions swallowed, no severities downgraded.

self_verification.notes: Every verdict is backed by a measured value (commit count, SHA comparison, message text, file content, git fsck output). Tests were run on independent scratch repos built from scratch by this QA agent — not using dev's reported numbers. The only deviation from the prompt is in Test 4 where 'SHA == original tip' was technically not achievable (orphaning a commit changes its SHA); we accept tree-SHA + message + metadata equivalence, which is the semantically correct interpretation.

summary_final.summary_line: iter2 fix verified: checkpoint-prune.sh now correctly keeps the NEWEST RETENTION commits. All 5 QA scenarios pass; L1-L5/O1/O2/O4/R1 unchanged; git fsck clean on production.
